column1: # ZAP scan rule overrides
# Format: ID
# Actions: IGNORE (suppress), WARN (report only), FAIL (break build)
# Docs: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/desktop/addons/alert-filters/
# --- Next.js / Vercel false positives ---
# Cache-control headers: Next.js and Vercel manage caching via their own
# mechanisms (CDN, ISR, stale-while-revalidate). ZAP flags these as missing
# but they are set correctly at the edge.
# Timestamp disclosure: Next.js build IDs embed timestamps. Not exploitable.
# Next.js embeds __NEXT_DATA__ JSON in pages which ZAP flags as information
# disclosure. This is by design and contains no sensitive server-side data.

column3: # Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments (Next.js build metadata)

column1: # CSP unsafe-inline in style-src: Next.js requires this without nonce-based CSP
# middleware (CSS-in-JS and Tailwind inject inline styles at runtime). Removing it
# would break the UI. The risk is limited compared to unsafe-inline in script-src.
# ZAP identifying the app as a Next.js SPA. Informational only, not a vulnerability.
# Next.js sets no-store on dynamic routes by default. Vercel handles edge caching.
# This is expected behaviour, not a misconfiguration.
# _vercel_sso_nonce is Vercel's own deployment protection cookie, not our session.
# Anti-CSRF tokens: Next.js server actions use built-in CSRF protection.
# ZAP may flag forms that don't use traditional token fields.
# --- ZAP probe / infrastructure false positives ---
# ZAP flags its own spider-injected test emails (e.g. zaproxy@example.com) appearing
# in URLs it constructed. Our forms submit via JS (onSubmit), not GET.

column3: # Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL

column1: # Retrieved from Cache: informational only — Vercel CDN caching is intentional.
# Cross-Domain Misconfiguration: Vercel CDN serves static assets with CORS *,
# which is expected and correct behaviour for public assets.
# Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: COEP requires all sub-resources to opt in via CORP/CORS.
# This would break Supabase and Vercel Analytics requests. Not applicable here.
# --- Weekly full-scan active-probe false positives ---
# CORS Misconfiguration (active probe): ZAP sends crafted Origin headers and flags
# permissive CORS responses. All instances hit _next/static/chunks — public, immutable,
# content-hashed assets served by Vercel CDN. API routes have no CORS headers set
# (same-origin policy applies). Verified: no Access-Control config in next.config.ts,
# middleware, API routes, or vercel.json.
# Proxy Disclosure (active probe): ZAP sends TRACE/OPTIONS to fingerprint proxies and
# detects Vercel's edge network. Inherent to any CDN-fronted deployment — not actionable.
# User Agent Fuzzer: informational active-scan check that confirms the app responds
# consistently to unusual User-Agent strings. Not a vulnerability.
